brand.name: KUM Services GmbH
brand.copyright: © 2025 KUM Services GmbH · All Rights Reserved

slides.description: KUM Services GmbH converts proven civil aircraft into customized platforms—delivering performance, reliability, and value faster than traditional programs.
Civil-registered platforms can ferry to your location and convert on-site—minimizing approvals, delays, and total time-to-operation.
From ISR and surveillance to cargo delivery and counter-UAS—configure the airframe to match mission needs, not the other way around.

approach.description: The conversion of civil aircrafts to flying drones is the easiest and fastest way. Civil aircrafts equipped for special mission are built for many different usage and customer requirements can be met at an affordable price.

whyUs.lead: There are a variety of manufacturers offering ready-made drone systems for purchase. These systems do not always meet customer requirements and are very costly with long delivery times. For this reason, it is important to offer a drone according to customer needs and available resources.

cards.title: Availability of Aircrafts
cards.description: Civil platforms are available on the market and can be selected based on mission and resource constraints.

cards.title: Low Cost
cards.description: A simple conversion path avoids lengthy development and construction timelines and keeps programme cost affordable.

cards.title: Design is Approved
cards.description: The base aircraft design is already approved and proven in civil operation.

cards.title: Reliability Proven
cards.description: Reliability is proven by thousands of flying hours, with worldwide maintenance options and spare parts supply.

callout.description: The aircrafts stay on civil registration and can be ferried to the place of operation with pilots. There is no need for special flight permits from authorities. Conversion to a drone takes about one to two days at the place of operation, and the aircraft is not identifiable as a drone.

aircraft.lead: Each platform is selected for operational suitability, global availability, and a proven civil-airframe track record.

aircrafts.name: CESSNA CITATION 525B
aircrafts.description: The Cessna Citation 525B is a light business jet with twin turbofan power, offering high cruise speed, high-altitude operation, and long range for rapid coverage and response. Civil-registered aircraft can be ferried to the operating location; conversion is typically completed on site within one to two days.
details: Well suited to missions requiring jet speed, FL450-class ceiling, and transcontinental range while retaining a standard civil-aircraft footprint.
Ideal where wide-area transit, time-sensitive repositioning, or high-altitude sensor lines of sight are required.
Mission equipment and avionics interfaces are engineered to your payload, certification path, and installation constraints.

aircrafts.name: CESSNA 208
aircrafts.description: The Cessna 208 is a high-wing single turboprop utility aircraft with a strong record in cargo, special mission, and austere-field operations. Its flexible cabin and large cargo door support sensors, mission equipment, and logistics payloads while keeping worldwide parts and maintenance access.
details: Strong short-field and unpaved-surface capability; well suited to logistics, surveillance, and multi-role mission kits.
Turboprop economics and global support networks keep operating cost predictable for sustained programmes.
Electrical, structural, and datalink interfaces are defined with your mission computers, sensors, and power budget.

aircrafts.name: C-130J
aircrafts.description: The Lockheed Martin C-130J Super Hercules is a four-engine turboprop tactical transport with very long range and a high-volume cargo hold. It suits the most demanding payloads and mission-system footprints where smaller platforms cannot deliver the required volume or endurance.
details: Exceptional range and payload for strategic lift, large sensor suites, or heavy mission equipment in a single airframe.
Rear loading and roll-on/roll-off flexibility support pallets, vehicles, and specialized mission racks.
Integration planning covers structural interfaces, power and cooling, EMI considerations, and certification-aligned milestones.

aircrafts.name: Beech King Air 350
aircrafts.description: The Beech King Air 350 is a pressurized twin turboprop with long endurance, stable cruise, and a proven multi-mission record. It is widely used for ISR, special missions, and executive transport, with global support and a mature maintenance ecosystem.
details: Flexible for sensors, communications, and operational mission kits.
We engineer integration to match your performance and maintenance expectations.
A strong option when you need endurance plus operational versatility.

applications.lead: Civil aircrafts converted to a drone can be used for the following operations.

cards.title: Cargo & Delivery
cards.description: Transportation and dropping of materials, possibly with a parachute.

cards.title: Defensive Operations
cards.description: Defense or attack of targets by dropping defensive measures.

cards.title: Range Extension
cards.description: Used as range extension system for cruise missiles.

partners.lead: University of Stuttgart and Part One-Forty Five GmbH provide academic and certified maintenance support for the programme.

items.label: EASA Part-145 Certified Maintenance & Repair Organisation
items.title: Part One-Forty Five GmbH
items.role: Patrick Kopfer · Neuhausen ob Eck Airfield
items.text: Part One-Forty Five GmbH is an authorized EASA PART-145 maintenance and repair organisation. The organisation maintains turbine powered turboprops and jets.

items.label: Academic Partner
items.title: University of Stuttgart
items.role: Institute of Flight Mechanics and Flight Control · Prof. Dr. Walter Fichter
items.text: The Institute of Flight Mechanics and Control (iFR) works in guidance, control and navigation of aerospace vehicles and instruments. Cirrus SR22 / D-EXPT is used as autonomous flight demonstration platform.

items.label: Lead Integrator
items.title: KUM Services GmbH
items.text: Prime contractor responsible for end-to-end project management, system integration, and customer delivery. Located at Haltnauer Weg 17, D-78464 Konstanz. Direct contact: +49 172 387 5262 · info@kum-trading.consulting

contact.lead: Describe your operational needs and we will prepare a tailored aircraft recommendation with full technical specification and commercial terms.

form.heading: Send an Enquiry
form.sub: All enquiries are handled with strict confidentiality. We typically respond within one business day.
form.note: Your information is treated with complete confidentiality and will not be shared with third parties.
